## Summary

Fixes issue #695 by making the PDF export path fault-tolerant instead of allowing PDFKit/rendering exceptions to surface as a browser HTTP 500 page.

## Changes

- Adds `toPdfSafeText(...)` for PDF-only text sanitization.
- Normalizes brittle Unicode/control characters before passing report/model text to PDFKit standard fonts.
- Replaces bullet/trademark/em dash/curly quote paths in PDF rendering with PDF-safe text.
- Wraps the PDF branch in a route-level `try/catch`.
- If PDF generation fails, returns a downloadable TXT fallback with `X-RevisionGrade-Export-Fallback: pdf-to-txt`.
- Logs `[report-download] PDF export failed` with job ID and error details for diagnosis.

## Scope

PDF export route only.

No TXT/DOCX changes intended, no evaluation scoring, no report synthesis, no pipeline, no job lifecycle, no auth/ownership, no database schema, no manuscript mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- `/api/reports/{completedJobId}/download?format=pdf` should no longer expose a raw HTTP 500 if PDF rendering throws.
- Normal PDF exports still return `Content-Type: application/pdf`.
- PDF-render failure returns status `200`, TXT content, and `X-RevisionGrade-Export-Fallback: pdf-to-txt`.
- Production logs include `[report-download] PDF export failed` only when fallback is used.

Closes #695.
